Title: I ordered from Boohoo and paid for next…
Text:
I ordered from Boohoo and paid for next day delivery, but my parcel was never even handed over to the courier. After contacting customer service, I was told to get back in touch if there was no update and a refund would be arranged. However, when I contacted them again, I was instead told that they needed to “investigate” the issue, leaving me still waiting with no parcel and no refund. I have now also been asked to wait a further two days for the ‘seller’ to respond, which is confusing as the issue appears to be entirely due to the seller’s actions. The lack of a timely resolution has made what should have been a straightforward process unnecessarily frustrating.

It’s extremely annoying to pay extra for next day delivery and then not receive the order at all, especially when the issue appears to be on Boohoo’s side. The communication has been inconsistent, and the whole process has taken far longer than it should. Very disappointing experience.

Title: Faulty shoes, zero accountability
Text: I purchased a pair of sneakers from ASOS for over £100. After only 5-6 wears within the first month, the outsoles deteriorated significantly — a clear manufacturing defect. When I contacted customer care they acknowledged the issue but refused to cover return postage despite being legally required to do so for faulty goods under the Consumer Rights Act 2015. I am currently abroad and they would not provide an international return label or offer any alternative remedy. After multiple escalations including to a manager, I was left with no resolution whatsoever. Extremely disappointing for a retailer of this size.

Title: STEALING FROM CUSTOMERS
Text:
If I could rate lower than 1 star I would. My experience with boohoo has been terrible! on the 10th of April 2026, I returned my boohoo order, a couple of days later Klarna reached out to me to advise boohoo have claimed to have not received this so I needed to provide proof of return. Several times this happened. I then spoke to Boohoo customer service via imessage, someone confirmed to me on the 23rd of April that they received my FULL return. On the 17th of May, I had an update on Klarna to advise I owed money- which is strange as I had returned all items and even had confirmation from boohoo they received this, I reached out to them again via imessage and was told there was nothing they could do as over a month had passed (SHOCKING!), I then emailed their customer service, which has been ongoing for a while now. On the 17th of May I received confirmation again that they have got my return and I was told to wait 8 working days for this to be investigated and for me to get my refund. 8 working days passed, I reached out to boohoo again and was told I received the refund in April (which is not correct as only part was returned to me), I told them to re-read my email trail and come back to me with an update, I was told I needed to wait a further 7 working days for it to be investigated...so what was the previous 8 working days for? I explained I cannot wait another 7 working days and needed an update and the full refund ASAP, I got a response saying it is being investigated and I need to wait 48 hours...
Customer service has been awful, I cannot believe you are refusing to give me my refund back even though you have confirmed on several occasions you received the full return. DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY UNLESS YOU WANT THEM TO STEAL YOUR MONEY.
I asked to speak to a manager to escalate my complaint and was asked 'why, there is nothing a manager can do that our admin team can't' well the admin team apparently can't even give me the refund I deserve. Not even a single apology, this has been going on for almost 2 months now. I will NOT be using Boohoo ever again and I will be taking this further.
I have been a loyal/regular customer with boohoo over the years and I am appalled at how I am being treated.

Title: Extremely Poor Customer Service and NO help for vulnerable people
Text:
**Extremely Poor Customer Service – Still Unable to Return £400+ Worth of Items**

I am extremely disappointed with the level of customer service I've received from Boohoo regarding a return worth over £400.

I followed the return process and selected multiple items to return. Boohoo originally sent my order in several separate parcels, yet I was only provided with one return code. When I attempted to arrange the return, I was later informed that I would need separate return codes for separate parcels. However, there appears to be no way to cancel or amend the original return request, leaving me completely stuck.

Despite contacting customer service multiple times, nobody has been able to resolve what should be a straightforward issue. The AI chat was unhelpful, and several customer service agents either failed to understand the problem or simply repeated that a return had already been created, despite me explaining multiple times that the issue was the inability to obtain the correct return labels. One response completely ignored the actual concern, and follow-up messages went unanswered.

My biggest concern is that while I continue trying to get help, I could end up outside the returns timeframe through no fault of my own. I have the items packed and ready to send back, but I cannot do so because Boohoo has not provided a workable solution.

As a long-standing customer, I expected far better support, particularly when trying to return such a significant order value. The lack of communication, ownership and urgency shown throughout this process has been incredibly frustrating.

I would strongly urge Boohoo to improve its returns process and ensure customer service agents are empowered to resolve issues rather than sending customers in circles.

I am heavily pregnant and about to be due on maternity leave so would be considered vulnerable under Consumer Duty and the Financial Ombudsman, something that boohoo doesn’t seem to be experienced in as I’ve been fobbed off at every communication possible. Feel so so let down and will not be shopping with them again unfortunately.

Title: Worst experience ever.
Text:
It would be Zero stars if this was an option...
Very disappointed with my experience after using Boohoo for many years. After returning several items from my order, my refund was not handled correctly, and I ended up paying more than I should have for the items I kept. I also incurred a late payment fee as a result of the issues with my account due to their delays.
What has been most frustrating is the way the matter has been handled. Resolving a straightforward refund issue has taken far more time and effort than it should have, and I have been left repeatedly chasing for answers rather than receiving clear, accurate support!
As a long-standing customer, I expected much better. Unfortunately, this experience has completely eroded my confidence in the company, and as a result, you have lost a valued customer.
There is a clear need for improvement in customer service, particularly when dealing with complaints and refund disputes. Customers deserve timely, knowledgeable, and personalised support from people who can properly investigate and resolve issues, rather than generic responses that fail to address the concerns being raised.
I cannot recommend a company that handles customer issues in this way and hope serious improvements are made to prevent others from having a similar experience. However, reading other reviews this is clearly a standard way of working. Be better Boohoo.

Title: Very disappointing experience with Oh…
Text:
Very disappointing experience with Oh Polly

On May 6th, I placed two orders, and unfortunately both experiences have been extremely frustrating.

The first order was never delivered to me, yet it was marked as “delivered”. As of May 30th, I still have not received the package, nor have I been refunded. I have contacted customer service multiple times, but they have not provided a solution or returned my money.

The second order consisted of two dresses that I pre-ordered for two very important events. At the time of purchase, I was informed that they would be delivered in May. However, at the last minute, I was notified that the delivery had been pushed back to July. Although I have the option to cancel the pre-order, the inconvenience has already been caused, as I specifically chose those dresses for those events and now have very little time to find suitable alternatives.

Overall, this has been a very disappointing experience. Poor communication, lack of accountability, and inadequate customer service have left me extremely dissatisfied. I would not recommend Oh Polly based on my experience.

Title: Total violation of consumer rights
Text:
Extremely poor experience with ASOS. They sent the wrong product (women’s trainers when not advertised as such), and despite me returning the item using their approved process with full proof of posting and confirmed delivery to their warehouse, they have refused to issue a refund. Customer service has been unhelpful and evasive, no email address or phone number, only a chat service which quickly and repeatedly deflects the issue onto an “account mismatch” with Apple ID sign-in rather than addressing the actual order and return evidence.

Communication is effectively non-existent beyond scripted chat responses, and my case was ultimately dropped without resolution. I now have no product, no refund, and no meaningful way to contact anyone who will take responsibility.

For a major retailer, this level of service is unacceptable.

Title: Extremely bad service!
Text:
Extremely bad service!

Lots of automated emails from Boohoo customer services for an online Debenhams sale. All extremely strange to be dealing with Boohoo customer service team for a Debenhams online sale!

Decided to buy a set of garden table and chairs from Debenhams online with delivery in time for when the set was needed. The item was shown at around £300 reduced to £160 in a sale. For the type of set there are a lot of very similar items online and even on Debenhams site now for around £300.

The evening before it was due to be delivered they cancelled the order stating a mistake had been made with the price online.

Turns out they have no right to cancel the order. If the price looks plausible (which it clearly did and based on a reduced sale price), they are bound by the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and have to honour the contract of sale.

They can only cancel if the "pricing error is obvious and unmistakable and which could reasonably have been recognised by you as a mispricing", but it was nowhere near obvious or mistakable, and just used a clause in the sale contract to suit themselves. We also had confirmation of the same by email confirming when the item would be delivered, so a contract is still in place.

Also turns out the Debenhams Group is not associated with an industry ombudsman, so you have no one to take your complaint to and seems to suit the way Debenhams go about their business.

Only form of recourse I have now is the small claims court which I am currently considering even if it leaves me out of pocket. Based on principles which is more than Debenhams have!

Refunded my money without me agreeing to it, and they tried to give me a voucher for money off another item but even the code they give me doesn't work. Not that I would go within a mile of this company now anyway!

Title: ASOS Expects Customers to Monitor Return Percentages Without Providing Transparency
Text:
ASOS’s new Fair Use Policy has completely changed the way I shop on their site.

I’m currently sitting at a 60% return rate and now feel anxious every time I place an order because I don’t know whether returning genuine sizing or fit issues could push me over the 70% threshold and affect my ability to renew Premier.

What frustrates me most is that the return rate is calculated over a rolling 12 months, even though this stricter policy only came into effect in February while my Premier membership started before that under completely different shopping expectations.

If customers had been reset to zero when the policy changed, I would have adjusted the way I shop much earlier. Instead, it feels like previous shopping behaviour is now being judged under a stricter system that didn’t exist at the time.

Online fashion shopping already involves guesswork. Different brands size differently, items don’t always look the same in person as they do online, and as someone tall and between sizes, returns are sometimes unavoidable.

The biggest issue for me is transparency. Customers are told to “monitor their return rate carefully”, but ASOS would not provide me with a proper account-specific breakdown showing exactly how my percentage had been calculated.

As a result, I now feel like I have to significantly reduce shopping with ASOS altogether to avoid being penalised.

I understand retailers need to manage excessive returns, but the way this has been introduced has completely changed my confidence in shopping with ASOS and no longer feels like a relaxed shopping experience.

Title: ASOS fails at inclusion
Text:
This isn't just an ASOS problem. It's a problem across much of the UK fashion industry. But ASOS is a perfect example.

ASOS sells thousands of men's clothing items. If you're a 28-inch waist, 30-inch waist, or need larger sizes all the way up to XXXXL and beyond, you'll find endless options. Entire pages of them.

But if you're a naturally slim man with a 26 or 27-inch waist? Good luck.

ASOS doesn't seem to sell shorts in my size at all. Heck the jeans are very rare. And everything else? Smart wear? Well you can dream on.. thats never happening.. for some bizarre reason...

Not one page. Not a small selection. Not even a handful of options hidden away somewhere. Just an endless wall of clothing that starts one or two sizes above where some of us actually exist.

What I don't understand is why the industry treats smaller men's sizes as if they're some kind of mythical creature. Being short and slim isn't unusual. There are plenty of men with smaller frames, yet retailers act as though anyone below a 28-inch waist simply doesn't need clothes.

The irony is that fashion brands rightly work hard to provide inclusive sizing at the larger end of the spectrum, but inclusivity seems to stop the moment you're a slimmer guy. Apparently it's completely normal to stock every size upwards, but not the sizes below.

I genuinely don't understand the logic. Why am I unable to buy clothes that fit me from one of the UK's biggest clothing retailers? Why is having a healthy, slim build treated like an edge case?

I'd love to buy your products. You just don't seem interested in selling them to people my size.

Title: Multi Vendor Orders - Poor Customer Service
Text:
I’ve had a really poor experience with ASOS returns, especially on a multi‑vendor order that included River Island. I followed River Island’s own return instructions and generated a separate return label, but the River Island label did not work at all. My ASOS label only allowed me to send one item back, so I ended up with two labels and no straightforward way to return everything. ASOS then issued another label and told me I’d be refunded once River Island confirmed delivery within 14 days. River Island later confirmed they had received the item, yet after 145 days I still had no refund and had to chase ASOS myself.

When I asked to raise a complaint, the agent (Tyron) said he had done so and gave me a complaint reference number, but I later found out there is no complaints process and the reference number wasn’t real. The next agent took nearly an hour to admit this and could only offer guesses instead of clear answers. Overall, the customer service has been very poor and it feels like customers have to prompt ASOS at every stage just to get basic things like working return labels and refunds processed. Title: Untrustworthy. Horrendous Customer Service.
Text:
I ordered a pair of running shoes from ASOS as they were cheaper than on competitor websites.
On arrival I assumed the trainers were the correct size 9 as they were in a box marked "size 9" and tied together with a label marked "size 9." It was only after I'd started running in them that it became apparent that, while the left trainer was correct, the right trainer was size 8 - too small.
I contacted ASOS by chat - they don't have email, phone or postal contact - and was told to return them via courier which I did.
Three weeks later I received the trainers back with a rejection email telling me they couldn't be returned due to their worn state.
I then tried to complain via chat and after 20 minutes with a handler I was told I would be put through to a manager. I then waited a further three hours before the connection was inexplicably cut.
I logged back in and opened another chat and was told I'd be prioritised and was asked to wait again. Thirty minutes later I was cut off once more.
Truly shocking service.

Title: ASOS experience
Text:
I genuinely want to know if anyone else has experienced this level of poor customer service from ASOS because this has honestly been exhausting.

For more than 5 working days, I’ve been trying to get help from ASOS regarding an account issue that THEY created. I originally stopped using ASOS back in 2020 after dealing with similar problems, but recently decided to give them another chance, hoping their service had improved. Big mistake.

After placing an order, a glitch signed me out of my account. When I tried logging back in, my account was suddenly blocked for no reason. Since then, I’ve spoken to 6 different customer service agents, and every single conversation has been exactly the same:

“Please check your junk/spam folder.”
“A specialist team will contact you.”

That’s it. No actual help. No updates. No accountability.

What makes it worse is that the chats are often ended midway through the conversation, right after I spend time explaining the issue in detail again and again. It genuinely feels like customers are being pushed in circles until they give up. At this point, I honestly question whether these are real support agents or just scripted AI responses repeating the same lines with zero empathy or understanding.

How is it acceptable for a customer to spend days chasing support just to access their own account and order? This isn’t customer service anymore, it feels like mental exhaustion by design. Customers are expected to repeatedly explain problems, wait endlessly, and receive no real resolution or even basic communication.

And this so-called “specialist team”? Apparently they are too “special” to provide updates, timelines, or even acknowledge what’s happening. According to the customer advisors, only the specialist team knows what’s going on, yet they never contact you. At this point, I’m starting to think this mysterious team doesn’t even exist.

If anyone else has experienced this kind of treatment from ASOS recently, please share your experience below because customers deserve better than this.

Title: Brief visit…
Text:
Bought 2 packs of boxer shorts for a family member.
They were deemed "unsuitable" and were left unopened. I returned the next day to my local Primark in Llandudno,in order to obtain a refund (£12). However, I was informed that refunds were not available on certain items which, included boxers. I explained that the items had not been unpacked, and certainly not worn, it having been less than 24hrs since they were purchased. Title: Title Title: Failure to uphold basic consumer‑rights obligations
Text:
I have been a loyal ASOS customer for 7–8 years, but my recent experience raises serious concerns about the company’s understanding and application of UK consumer law.

I purchased a pair of Mango black heels for an event. After less than an hour of normal wear, the straps snapped, rendering the shoes unusable. I contacted ASOS and was instructed to return the item for inspection, which I did. A week later, the shoes were returned to me with no refund and the explanation that they could not assist because the shoes had been “worn”.

This response is fundamentally inconsistent with the Consumer Rights Act 2015, which states that goods must be of satisfactory quality, fit for purpose, and durable. An item that fails within an hour of use is, by definition, faulty, and the consumer is entitled to a refund.

I spent three hours speaking to multiple customer‑service agents, repeatedly explaining the legal position. Only after explicitly referencing the legislation and stating that the shoes were not fit for purpose did one agent finally acknowledge the issue and process the refund.

While I appreciate the individual who ultimately resolved the matter, the overall experience demonstrates a clear lack of training and understanding of statutory consumer protections. Customers should not have to quote legislation to receive the basic rights afforded to them under UK law.

Extremely disappointing from a retailer of ASOS’s scale.

Title: Very poor customer service!!!
Text:
Very poor customer service!!!

Firstly, why is there a bot handling issues? Every time an agent finally joins the chat, they send one quick message and then disappear. When I reply, there is no response, and I’m forced to start all over again answering the same automated bot questions. This is utterly ridiculous and extremely frustrating.

My issue is that I received my Boohoo refund as store credit. This happened because store credit is set as the default option when requesting a return. I would never have knowingly selected credit, as I paid using Klarna and now have an outstanding payment due.

Yes, I did not manually change the option, however under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013, refunds must be issued to the original payment method unless the customer has expressly agreed otherwise. A pre-selected default option does not constitute valid consent.

I know many other customers have experienced this exact same issue, which makes this appear to be a deliberate company tactic. I find this completely unacceptable and I am appalled by both the lack of transparency and the poor customer service I have received.

I demand a refund to my original payment method immediately. This is unacceptable, and if it is not resolved, I will escalate this further as this is misleading and a breach of consumer rights. I expect action, not another automated response!

We appreciate this work around make incur additional shipping charges, however we'd be more than happy to cover these charges on the new order. Just pop back to the team once submitted and we'll get these refunded for you.

Many thanks again for taking the time to feed this back to us.
All the best
Lucy & Yak

Title: Boohoo's refund policy is a scam
Text:
Boohoo's refund voucher policy is a blatant dark pattern.

I accepted the default gift voucher refund, only to discover later (via social media complaints) that it expires after just 90 days - a term buried in the returns page and not stated on my gift card email, in the main gift card T&Cs, or in the FAQs.

Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015, terms should be transparent and fair. This is neither. The CMA should take note.

Title: This is the SECOND time Boohoo has…
Text:
This is the SECOND time Boohoo has failed to deliver an order under their so-called “next day delivery” service. At this point, it feels completely misleading to advertise a service they clearly cannot provide consistently. Customers are paying extra for delivery promises Boohoo repeatedly fails to keep.

What makes it worse is the shocking level of customer service. Facebook Messenger was apparently the only contact option, and I was asked THREE times to send screenshots, only to then be told they “can’t receive images.” So after wasting my time, I had to type everything out manually instead. I was then told there would be a long wait for a reply… and they still never responded.

Boohoo seems far more interested in taking customers’ money than actually providing the service they advertise or offering proper support when they fail. Delayed orders happen, but poor communication, broken customer service systems, and ignoring customers afterwards is unacceptable.

If Boohoo cannot handle next day delivery properly, they should stop offering it altogether instead of misleading customers into paying for something they are unlikely to receive.

Title: a return to clothing as a form of respect
Text:
'There are garments one wears… and then there are garments one enters into relationship with.

My experience with Rapanui has been of the latter.

I came to them quietly, seeking not simply comfort, but congruence — a harmony between what touches my skin and what I hold to be true. Their pieces, particularly the underwear and trackies I now find myself living in, carry a rare integrity. The fabric does not feel manufactured in haste; it feels considered, almost reverent. There is a softness that is not merely tactile, but energetic — as though the material itself has been allowed to remain close to its origin.

Rapanui’s commitment to organic fibres, circular design, and renewable production is not just branding rhetoric; it is perceptible. One senses it in the breathability of the cotton, in the absence of harshness, in the way the garments settle on the body without resistance. It is clothing that does not impose — it accompanies.

In a world saturated with synthetic urgency, there is something profoundly grounding about wearing pieces that feel clean in both composition and intention. For those of us who are attentive to the subtle dialogue between body, environment, and energy, this matters deeply. What we wear becomes an extension of our field.

I have worn their underwear and trackies not as occasional comforts, but as daily companions — and they have held their shape, their softness, and their quiet presence with grace. Others speak similarly of their quality and natural materials, often highlighting their durability and comfort, alongside a genuine commitment to sustainability.

This is not fast fashion. It is something slower, more conscious — a return, perhaps, to clothing as a form of respect: for the body, for the earth, and for the unseen threads that connect the two.

For me, purity in clothing is not an aesthetic preference; it is a practice. And in that regard, Rapanui feels aligned.'

Title: ASOS Customer Experience Has Seriously Degraded And Failed On Every Level
Text:
I have been ordering from ASOS for many years and never had such a disappointing experience before.

My order was never delivered due to a confirmed issue with the shipping provider. I received no delivery attempt, no notification, and completely contradictory information from customer service for days. At one point I was told the parcel was still on the way, while another agent confirmed it was already being returned.

I spent 2 hours in multiple chats trying to get a clear explanation. I was promised a callback within 3 working days starting from Monday, but nobody contacted me within the stated timeframe.

My direct questions regarding the actual reason for the failed delivery and the reason why any goodwill compensation was being refused were constantly avoided instead of being answered clearly.

One agent even ended the chat without resolving the issue or addressing my remaining questions.

The most frustrating part is that the items are now out of stock, meaning I lost the opportunity to receive products I specifically ordered. Despite acknowledging problems with the shipping process and communication, ASOS refused any goodwill gesture or compensation.

Unfortunately, this entire situation explains many of the negative Trustpilot reviews about their customer service.

I never thought I would have such an experience with ASOS after being a customer for so many years. However, this situation made it more than obvious that ordering from their website is no longer worth it and that communication with customer care is practically nonexistent.

The customer service agents seemed unable to properly read messages, answer direct questions, or suggest actual solutions. At this point, I honestly question what the purpose of ASOS Customer Care even is.

What makes this even more absurd is that one week earlier I was explicitly asked in which form I would expect a goodwill compensation. Later, every following agent suddenly denied that any form of goodwill compensation was possible at all.

I will definitely never order from ASOS again, despite the fact that I started ordering from them back when I was still in school — and that was already around 10 years ago.

This feels like a clear degradation of the overall customer experience and obvious cost-cutting on customer support resources — something that ultimately harms the business itself and drives away loyal customers.

Absolutely terrible experience.

Title: Absolutely shocking customer service…
Text:
Absolutely shocking customer service from Boohoo. Avoid.

I returned items back in February:
- Tracksuit returned 11th February
- Shoes returned 13th February

Both were successfully delivered back to them and I have proof of postage and delivery, yet I am STILL chasing my refund now in May.

I should have received £55.48 back in total. After months of chasing, emailing and repeating myself over and over again, I was finally sent one £34 gift card. I am STILL missing the rest of my refund.

What makes this even worse is the completely inconsistent information I’ve been given by different customer service agents:
- One told me the voucher had expired
- Another told me a new one would be sent within 24 hours (it wasn’t)
- Then another claimed the voucher had already been spent

Every single response contradicts the last one. It honestly feels like nobody reads previous messages and they just send copy-and-paste replies to get rid of customers.

I have spent MONTHS chasing money that is legally owed to me. No customer should have to repeatedly beg for their own refund after returning items correctly.

The lack of communication, accountability and organisation is unbelievable. Looking online now, I can see so many people experiencing the exact same thing, which is really concerning.

I used to order from Boohoo regularly, but after this experience I genuinely will not shop here again.

If you return items, be prepared to:
- Wait months for refunds
- Constantly chase customer service
- Receive conflicting information
- Still not get your full money back

Absolutely unacceptable.

Title: Avoid choosing the “refund gift card…
Text:
Avoid choosing the “refund gift card option”, even with the extra 10-20% offered. Boohoo are extremely slow at issuing the gift card, it took longer than a normal refund to my bank account, and you only get 90 days to use it.
Another thing to be aware of: if you select a gift‑card refund for any item in your return, the entire return is automatically switched to a gift card and you can’t change it back.

In my case, I already had items I wanted to repurchase, but because the gift card was issued so late, those items went out of stock before I even received it. Over the 3‑month window, they never came back in stock and there was very little available I wanted to buy. The gift card has now just expired when there are items I want, so I’ve effectively lost my own money by choosing that option and you’ll probably need more than 3 months to locate something to utilise that gift card for and boohoo won’t extend by a couple of days if you do find something just outside that 3 month expiry.

Going forward, I’ll always choose a standard refund so I’m not restricted by a short time limit or left unable to buy items because they’ve sold out before the gift card arrives. While also being out of pocket by the dreadful offer and process of issuing for this refund gift card as boohoo aren’t quick to issue it. Therefore there is no real benefit in gaining the gift card over actual refund.

Title: Been an ASOS customer for a long time…
Text:
Been an ASOS customer for a long time and generally happy with the products — good range, competitive prices and returns are straightforward. However recently noticed a real issue with how delivery information is presented at the point of purchase.
Ordered a pair of trainers only to find the expected delivery date is 16 days away. It turns out the item is not in stock but this is buried in small print and far from obvious when you're placing your order. For a retailer of ASOS's size that's not good enough — if an item isn't in stock that should be front and centre, not hidden away where most people won't notice it.
Also noticed that selecting free delivery seems to come with significantly slower dispatch times generally. Fine if you're aware of it but again not made particularly clear upfront.
Long term customer but this kind of thing erodes trust. Be sure to read the small print carefully before ordering, especially on footwear.

Title: Bad customer service and no process for simple mistakes

Not Boohoo though.... because they 'so helpfully' send the orders straight to warehouse for quick dispatch, there's absolutely no way for them to do anything at all. Text:

I have formally reported ASOS Germany GmbH to the Finanzamt Berlin-Neukölln (Tax Office) for regulatory review. Do not buy from here if you require valid tax documentation.

Title: Fast checkout, slow delivery, zero accountability
Text:
I recently shopped on the ASOS app ahead of a holiday and needed some last-minute essentials. The browsing and checkout experience itself was smooth, and I selected next-day delivery, happy to pay the £5.99 given the urgency.
At checkout, I was prompted to upgrade to ASOS Premier for £9.95, which offered unlimited next-day delivery. Since I expected to place more orders, I agreed and paid for the subscription.
However, immediately after checkout, I received a notification saying my delivery was estimated in 8 days — completely defeating the purpose of paying for next-day delivery.
I contacted customer support to resolve the issue, and the experience was extremely disappointing. The team showed little interest in helping and even questioned whether I had actually signed up for Premier. I had to send screenshots of my own account as proof. Despite this, they refused to correct the delivery speed or refund the Premier fee.
Overall, the app experience was fine, but the customer service was poor and unhelpful when it mattered most. Based on this experience, I would not shop with ASOS again.

Title: Next day delivery is a myth
Text:
I have consistently experienced issues with ASOS next day delivery. On every occasion I have selected this option, my parcel has failed to arrive on time.

Despite being aware of this pattern, I recently placed another order and paid extra for the premium next day service, which clearly stated delivery before 12 to 1pm. As expected, the order did not arrive .

This level of unreliability is unacceptable, especially when customers are paying a premium for a guaranteed service. The core issue is not just the delay, but the misleading nature of the delivery promise. If next day delivery cannot be fulfilled consistently, that should be clearly communicated at the point of purchase.

Customers often organise their day around these delivery windows. When those commitments are not met, it causes unnecessary disruption and inconvenience.

Based on repeated experiences, I will not be using this service again.

Title: ASOS are not keeping customers safe
Text:
Asos do not care about their customers. Their insistence on using a courier - Evri - who are confirmed by the ICO as non-GDPR compliant and in direct breach of GDPR law every single day puts all their customers data at risk.

Asos are sending your name and address to a company who does not follow or respect privacy laws. Who is Evri then selling your private information to? How secure are they keeping it when they don’t follow GDPR law - as confirmed by the ICO, the actual government body in charge of fining and prosecuting businesses who breach GDPR law?

Asos will continue to send all your data to them though, rather than find a new courier to use. Evri will also lose all your parcels and items anyway and breach consumer law constantly. So it’s not even worth shopping on ASOS until they stop using Evri. Get your privacy abused but don’t receive your items? No thanks.

Asos don’t care about the abuses Evri commit against their customers with every single order. So how much do they care about their customers?

The answer is: ZERO.

I will continue to share negative feedback about ASOS as long as they continue to use Evri. At this point, ASOS have the responsibility to keep customers privacy rights safe and they’re not doing so. I’ve told them directly and sent them the proof from the ICO showing that Evri are in breach of and continuing to breach privacy laws for ASOS customers and ASOS have replied and acknowledged it - so ASOS is fully aware that they are sending your private, personal data to a company who could misuse it. Yet they continue to send your info over - regardless of how unsafe it is.

Consider this and shop elsewhere.

Title: Happy to break consumer law...
Text:
Every step of my experience with Boohoo from the point of delivery onwards was shambolic. One of my two items had a massive hole and the other's clasp system was completely unfit for purpose. However, these things happen and I was prepared to just return the items and move on, but Boohoo had other ideas.

In order to waive the return fee, (which of course I shouldn’t have to pay given that the items were faulty), I had no option but to contact their customer support, (and honestly, they should consider renaming this because it does everything bar support the customer). Their messaging system is completely inefficient and unfit for purpose – it took 36 hours for a human being to reply to me (with a query, not a conclusion) and a total of nine days of very confusing toing and froing to reach the very unclear conclusion that the return fee had been waived for both items. During this back and forth I was asked to repeat the same information over and over again, told I couldn’t send video evidence, only to be asked to send video evidence, and told I was within the return date but not entitled to a full refund but with no justification as to why; (this one went on and on for several days).

Once all of this was concluded and the returns sent, I assumed this would be the matter resolved, but alas, I was very, very wrong. Having had no notification or record of my return from Boohoo, (though of course I retained proof of sending), I figured I could afford them the benefit of the doubt for a little while. However, after six weeks with no updates, I did a little digging in which I confirmed that a) my return had been received two days after sending and (more importantly) b) by law, sellers must refund a buyer within 14 days of receipt of the return. Boohoo’s flagrant disregard for this law is shocking and, diving into this on social media it would appear that I am not an anomaly – this is just their MO.

I emailed them to express my understanding of consumer rights and threatening to escalate this further, (which I still intend to), and in a matter of hours received my refund. In a way, this is even more incriminating as it appears they are preying on people being uneducated on their rights (and the law) to withhold funds and then are only inclined to act accordingly once threatened.

This company are farcical and are clearly very comfortable in pushing legal boundaries for their own benefit. I couldn’t recommend them any less.

Title: Missing parcel, false delivery attempt, fake investigation, and complete disregard for consumer law
Text:
I ordered from ASOS on 14.04 and the parcel was never delivered. The delivery provider falsely claimed an “attempted delivery” on 17.04 — I was home all day, nobody rang the door, and no delivery was attempted. When I went to the pick‑up point myself, the parcel was not there. The parcel is clearly missing.

ASOS then told me an “investigation” had been raised and would take 48–72 hours. After waiting, another ASOS worker (Shivdutt) openly admitted that the investigation had never even been started, despite me providing screenshots of previous colleagues confirming it was already underway. So not only was the delivery attempt fake — the investigation was too.

Instead of taking responsibility, ASOS keeps insisting I wait indefinitely, even though the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013 make the retailer legally responsible for ensuring delivery within the agreed timeframe. Once the delivery deadline is missed and the parcel is not delivered, the consumer is entitled to a refund. ASOS is refusing to comply with this basic legal obligation.

I will have to escalate this through my bank and Citizens Advice because ASOS will not follow consumer law, will not take ownership, and cannot even keep their own story straight.

This is unacceptable from a major retailer. Fake delivery attempt, missing parcel, contradictory information from staff, a non‑existent investigation, and a refusal to issue a lawful refund. I will not be ordering from ASOS again.

Title: Just a warning for anyone shopping with…
Text:
Just a warning for anyone shopping with ASOS, be very careful with returns.

I ordered several dresses for an event, tried them on the same day they arrived, and selected a few to keep. The rest were returned the next day.

Out of the returned items, one dress was flagged as worn and my refund was refused. There were no photos and no proper explanation, just a claim.

This makes no sense. The dresses did not fit me properly, they were only tried on briefly, and returned immediately. There was no opportunity to wear them. I followed the returns process correctly and in good faith.

Now I am being sent back an expensive dress I will never wear, based on an unproven accusation.

My advice. Take photos when you receive your items. Take photos before you return them. Keep proof of everything.

If something goes wrong, you may be blamed without evidence.

Very disappointing experience. This was my last purchase with ASOS. I will not be dealing with this kind of hassle again.

Title: Dear Lucy & Yak Team,
Text:
Dear Lucy & Yak Team,

I wanted to share my heartfelt thanks for the genuinely wonderful experience I had at your paint workshop in the Spitalfields Market pop-up on Friday 23rd January @ 11-1.

From the moment I arrived, your team made me feel so welcome — especially Joe, the store manager, and Marina, his cheerful and approachable assistant. When the workshop started a little late, Marina immediately put us at ease, invited us to look around the beautiful store and greeted everyone with a warm, friendly smile.

When Joe arrived, he couldn’t have been more apologetic and attentive. He took the time to explain how the workshop would run, asked if we needed anything, and listened when I explained how nervous I was about painting on my special work dungarees. Rather than dismissing my concern, he kindly offered me a practice tote bag, even asking what colour I preferred — such a thoughtful touch.

His reassuring, patient, and genuinely caring manner — along with bringing us paints and offering cold drinks — made the afternoon truly special. Joe checked in at just the right moments to be supportive without hovering. I had the best couple of hours; I felt confident, creative, and genuinely happy — something I really needed after a tough week.

This experience has given me the confidence to personalise my Lucy & Yak dungarees and aprons with my own designs, and I couldn’t be more delighted with what I created. See photos attached.

Please could you pass this message on to head office and to Joe, and let him know how much his kindness and encouragement meant to me? I’d also love for Marina to be recognised for her warm, bright energy — her smile really sets the tone for a welcoming experience.

Hi Julianna

We understand how upsetting it will have been to believe you were shopping with us, only to find out it was a fraudulent website. Sadly, as the fraudulent website is out of our control, the team are limited in the options we have in resolving this for you. However, we'd recommend reaching out to your bank as they’re much better placed in dealing with fraudulent payments and scams, and should be able to reimburse you based on the evidence you can submit to them showing that this site was fraudulent.

There have been a number of fraudulent sites impersonating us by using our images and content to mislead our customers - we are working super hard to make sure these sites are reported and taken down where possible. Some of the ways in which we have been able to identify these sites is the URL website address, which when shopping with us should only ever be lucyandyak.com, and the pricing of the items on the site. If the price appears too good to be true, it unfortunately usually is.

We really do appreciate how the conflicting information from the team has further confused matters, we will be reviewing these with the team directly to make sure this is addressed internally, as we want to ensure we are giving the best experience with our customer service.

We have popped over an email directly so we can discuss things further.
Hope to speak with you soon,
Lucy & Yak Team

Title: Was an avid customer for years but sadly Ive given up: terrible sizing and iffy pricing. This feels like a break-up 😆
Text:
Hey Clair,

Thanks so much for your feedback - we really appreciate your enthusiasm for our brand and it's great to see that you've had a good experience with our customer service, but we're sorry to hear that you've had the issues mentioned in your review!

We can understand that your main frustration is coming from our sizing - including the size charts and body measurements, differences between intended fit of different items and the adaptation of styles from 'old fit' to 'new fit' over time. This feedback is really valuable for us as we're always working to improve our customer experience and we want the information available on our product pages to be useful and accurate to ensure our customers can get the right fit first time.

We understand that finding the right size when you're shopping online can be tricky, especially when we've made changes based on feedback over the years (like the old and new fit changes you've mentioned.) This is part of the reason we usually provide body measurements in our size charts, rather than the garment measurements. The feedback we've received in the past has shown that customers find it easier to find the fit that looks like the model in the online image using these body measurements, rather than comparing the garment measurements to their own and misjudging the intended fit (whether oversized or fitted). However - we can totally see where more information on the product page to allow you to choose how you'd prefer the item to fit would be helpful - including whether that particular item is intended to be roomy (like a baggy jumper or slouchy dungas) or fitted (like our Ragan jumpsuit!)

We'll make sure your feedback is passed on to our garment team, as we've recently been making plenty of changes behind the scenes to improve the fit and sizing experience, so if we can implement any solutions that would help address the issues you've raised, we'd love to do so.

As for our pricing and sales - while we completely understand where you're coming from, businesses like ours naturally need to add items to sale from time to time in order to clear stock - especially at the end of seasons, and there will always be someone who purchased the item at full price. The price of organic cotton is on the rise due to increased demand (which is great news for more sustainable fabrics on the market!), so some of our prices are higher than they used to be - though we make a concerted effort to keep our costs as affordable as possible while still remaining committed to looking after people and planet. As for feeling a little like fast fashion, we always appreciate feedback on this kind of issue as we work hard to strike a balance between being conscious of our impact on the planet and on how our customers are shopping with us, while creating eye-catching, comfortable items that feel accessible for our existing customers and inspire more people to join a community that offers a more conscious alternative. You can check out more about our stance on maintaining this balance at our blog post below, which hopefully does a good job of explaining where we're coming from in terms of focusing on circularity, reinvesting and running our business responsibly;
https://lucyandyak.com/blogs/news/fast-fashion-vs-slow-fashion-why-were-neither

If there's anything else we can help with, or if you'd like to contact our small dedicated positive change team you can reach them at PositiveChange@lucyandyak.com. They collect any feedback from our community and share it with the relevant departments and/or our advisory panel each month. All the best, Lucy & Yak

Title: London
Text:
I bought my dungarees at the sale in London this year.
I went with my daughter who is a total convert.
Now, at the age of 72, I have had breast cancer for the second time in 22 years.
Post mastectomy and reconstruction, as I am healing, I have found the dungarees to be a blessing. They are both comfortable and easy to remove and put on.
Go Lucy and Yak.
I have a soap box......
Over 70s don't neglect mamagrams! One third of people who get diagnosed with breast cancer are over 70. Nag friends and relatives.
Being a woman is not easy, but we are not HELPLESS.
Madelaine

Title: Lucy & Yak have changed alot recently
Text:
Hey Erin

We appreciate there have been lots of changes within Lucy & Yak over the past few years - these have been from Customer feedback and making some necessary improvements to our current processes.

The closure of our Rewards programme was to make room for an exciting alternative - You & Yak. You & Yak is our new loyalty programme, designed by us for all of you, our Lucy & Yak community. It’s a space to bring you all together to connect and enjoy our Pink Perks. Whether it’s getting your order totally refunded, previews of future collections, invites to our exclusive events or early access to our Limited Edition products, there's lots to come through the year. If you can drop us a message at hello@lucyandyak.com, we may be able to check your previous account to see if there are any unused vouchers to redeem for you so you don't miss out!

We've recently introduced a new system for our returns, where return labels are free for exchanges and store credit to thank you for continuing to shop with Lucy & Yak. However, there is a small fee of £2.50 to create a returns label for cash refunds. This cost is due to the increase in postage costs over the last couple of years - we hope you're understanding of our efforts to provide multiple return options and keep costs as low as possible for you!

We try to make sure we keep our customers up to date with any changes we make but we will absolutely pass on your feedback to try to ensure we give as much notice as possible where we can.

Please do let us know if we can do anything else for you or if you have any questions,
All the best
Lucy & Yak

Title: “Don’t Charge Us to Advertise for You!”
Text:
I am outraged by Primark’s decision to start charging 15p for paper bags. This isn’t about sustainability; it’s an obvious money grab at the expense of customers. These bags are flimsy, tear easily, and have your branding plastered all over them—essentially, we’re being asked to pay for the privilege of being walking billboards for your company.

This charge feels completely unjustified. Customers should never be forced to pay for something so low-quality and disposable. If you truly cared about sustainability, you would offer durable, reusable bags, not flimsy ones that break after a single use. Charging customers for these bags preys on our trust, and it’s clear that this is just another way to increase profits under the guise of “helping the environment.”

What’s even worse is that this unfair practice is now becoming common across retailers. But I refuse to accept this as the new normal. As customers, we need to stand up and reject any company that tries to profit off us by charging for bags that have no real value. Primark should be ashamed of this decision, and I will no longer shop at a store that disrespects its customers this way.

I strongly encourage everyone to stop supporting this exploitative practice. Don’t let Primark—or any other retailer—think they can get away with charging us for something that should be part of our shopping experience without an extra cost. Refuse to accept it. Speak up. Demand better.

Title: Disappointing Checkout Experience at Primark Llanelli
Text:
Visited the Llanelli Primark store recently to pick up some clothing. The shopping experience started off well—the range of clothes was reasonable, and the changing rooms were spacious and easy to access.

However, things took a sharp downturn at the checkout.

The entire checkout process was poorly designed and frustrating. The area appears to have been recently revamped, but not for the better. There was only one staff member handling sales and returns at a very small counter, and another stationed at a separate area where customers were funneled into a queue to use self-service checkouts.

The self-checkouts themselves were confusing and inconsistent—some had handheld scanners, others did not. Trying to scan clothes, pay extra for a bag, and locate a printed receipt turned into a chaotic task. To make matters worse, as we attempted to exit, we were abruptly stopped and spoken to rudely by a staff member. Apparently, you’re required to scan your receipt in order to leave the area—something that was never made clear during the process.

It left a sour taste after what had been a decent in-store experience. A little more signage, clearer instructions, and better staffing could make a world of difference. Until then, be prepared for a stressful finish to your shopping trip.

Title: DO NOT ORDER
Text:
I've shopped with People Tree over the years and never had any problems... Until July 2023. I ordered 2 dresses but had to send them back because the fabric was really thin and they didn't fit properly. More Primark than People Tree quality. At the end of July, I noticed that the refund had not been processed and emailed People Tree.

On July 31 I got a reply from the company Director, he said that their customer service specialist had had to leave the company at short notice and that “we are working through the backlog of outstanding issues as fast as we can to ensure that nothing gets missed whilst we recruit and train someone else.”

Fast forward to September. A large letter landed on my doormat informing me that People Tree UK has collapsed into administration and listing me as one of the creditors. Unfortunately, I paid by debit card and never got my money back.

The reason I’m posting this review is because I can see that their European website is still running and I saw two reviews from EU customers who ordered but never received anything. DO NOT ORDER!!! The European website hasn’t been updated for months. If you look at T&C’s, you’ll see that the website is operated by People Tree Ltd, a company registered in the UK under number 02949366, whereas orders are fulfilled by People Tree Enterprises BV, a company registered in The Netherlands under KVK 82275602.

People Tree Ltd, registration number 02949366, is the company that entered administration last September. How can a company that is being liquidated operate a website and forward information about orders to the Dutch arm of People Tree? You can find Opus Restructuring Llp (their liquidator) details on Companies House should you wish to contact them.

If you’re reading this and are EU based, please spread awareness so that unsuspecting customers wouldn’t be placing new orders on the Dutch website! On September 27, 2023 The Guardian wrote that “People Tree’s European website is continuing to operate, with a note promising that shipping to the UK will begin soon, but former insiders said the European operation’s only employee was being made redundant and it was not clear how trade would be able to continue as the buying and marketing had been run from the UK.”

Title: Poor Service, Awful In-store Experience - Why bother when there is better
Text:
I was told if I was to contact customer services that you would be able to help. This week I tried to return some items that I bought before my holidays and I was refused the return or exchange for items I had taken to the till due to them being slightly outside the 28 return date.

I explained to the store attendant and then supervisor that I am 28 weeks pregnant and have been poorly and unable to return these items. The items I wanted to return total between £50- 60 which is a lot to lose with items that don't fit me and a few that didn't fit my children. As you are aware there is a bit of a culture with Primark that encourages us to spend freely with lower prices, however I am so disappointed that I have done that and been met with such a strict returns policy. As a bit of insight I would like to say I was 25 weeks pregnant when I bought these items, your changing rooms on the women's wear level were closed, the store was extremely hot, staff directed me to other changing rooms that were just as busy and then then I queued for about 30 minutes to make my purchase. Its really hard and a challenge to shop there when you are not pregnant never mind pregnant.

At the very least I was only expecting to exchange - no more but I travelled all the way to Warrington to have a wasted journey and it just really upset me as other retailers (like New Look) didn't even ask they just did the returns without a query. I was even more disappointed that the human factor didn't exist to understand the challenges as a pregnant woman in your store has had and why I just bought items without trying on.

With other retailers relaxing their returns policy, some going to unlimited! It’s clear that it encourages repeat purchases that are far more valuable.

PRIMARK VALUE NO ONE just want you in and out of their stores and your money. Zero service. Get to a better shop, plenty that have better quality online and in stores like new look that deliver to your door and don’t have this issue. First store that I’ve ever known not to even exchange with a receipt even after the date! It was a day or two!

Title: Non-colourfast dyes; they run, they stain!
Text:
Decent stores, nice staff (where I've visted). The clothes are unsurprisingly very bad and not for the reason you might first expect.

The problem is the dyes. The stitching of clothes is usually great and the materials are fairly good, but none of the garments have good quality fast dyes. End result? After a few washes your garment fades, or worse; if it is black, navy or dark grey it will end up with brownish red stains where the red dye has run and the others haven't. This actually makes Primark terrible value for money as you'd need to replace every garment that does this far more often than if you'd paid double the price elsewhere. Think, one pair of shorts from Primark lasting a month or two versus a well made pair with good dyes lasting one or two years, perhaps longer.

I won't shop here again. I don't shop here exclusively, but this is one of the few stores I keep having to buy replacements for. Matalan was the same. Cheap dyes ruin otherwise good clothing. Bad value. Bad quality non-colourfast dyes. There really is no point stepping foot in this shop.

Note: all washed per the label unstructions (40°C) & washed with similar colours, inside out.

Title: Terrible refund policy
Text: I shop in Primark a lot and generally the quality of the clothes is good and better priced than other high street retailers, although the sizing of their clothes is all over the place often meaning I have to go up two sizes in jeans - that’s fine I expect it now, but their refund policy is shocking. I bought some jeans that didn’t fit and a bodysuit that was too short even though it was the right size on the label, I’ve searched everywhere for the receipt and can’t find it. Their refund policy is that you have to have the *original till receipt* not *proof of purchase* as you do with other retailers. Sometimes people lose their receipts, life happens! Luckily the value of my items I wanted to return was only £16 but if I were a person in financial hardship like so many people are these days, this would be a lot of money to suck up! Title: Wrong item sent twice

Dear People Tree,

Imagine my surprise then, to find that the item sent out to me was the wrong one again. In fact, exactly the same wrong one I had sent back.
I now have to go through this ridiculous process again. What is going on People Tree? I know there's a pandemic, but this feels beyond careless.
What a pity. I won't be shopping with you again.

Title: Outstanding clothing for people with Autistm, sensory and/or allergy sensitivities
Text: I have received two orders over the past 2 months from People Tree. The garments are wonderful! I have Asperger's and people on the spectrum can have great difficulty with clothing because of our sensory sensitivities. Anything from clothing tags, seams, fabric texture, fit, reactions to synthetic materials can all create a high level of discomfort akin to having severe dry itchy skin or a sunburn (strange, I know). People Tree is the first company where I can wear anything and nothing hurts. The garments I've purchased are organic cotton (regular, brushed, and flannel) and Tencel. I look forward to trying their Linen garments as I'm a big fan of flax as an eco-friendly crop. I originally wanted to buy from PT because they stand for ethical slow fashion, human rights, and environmental protection. They care. But being able to wear clothing that doesn't hurt me is an incredible experience. Thank-you PT for (unintentionally?!) making Autistic friendly garments. On to the shipping experience (amid a pandemic): The first order saw some complications regarding delayed shipping and unanswered emails which led to a lot of frustration and irate emails on my end however it got resolved and as a gesture of goodwill, PT refunded the shipping fee (shipping outside UK). The second order saw an older item (reiko trousers) out of stock in my size and the shipment didn't go out 'right away' because the CSR wanted to make absolute certain that the trousers were, in fact, out of stock. I appreciated that. It was confirmed they were out of stock and a refund was processed same day. Highly recommend PT. They have some hiccups, for sure, but they're human beings open to constructive feedback and, well, Rome wasn't built in a day.

Title: Quick to respond to emails and to refund returns
Text:
I ordered a pair of trousers from People Tree that were the wrong size and which I ended up having to return. I had a discount code for my first purchase and as People Tree don't do exchanges I had to pay full price for my new order. However, I contacted People Tree about this and they quickly replied and refunded me the difference. When my orders arrived I found the trousers to be wrapped in plastic which was a little disappointing as one of the main reasons that I chose People Tree is because of their environmental and fairtrade ethos. However, when I queried this I was assured that the plastic is indeed recyclable. I suggest that People Tree make this clearer as no doubt this question has, and will continue to be raised many times and without symbols on the bags, how are we to know?

When I read the reviews of this company on Trust Pilot after making my orders I was a little worried as it seems like a lot of people have had problems with returns making it back into their bank accounts. However, my return was quick, as was the communication and my trousers are excellent quality that are clearly made to last. I will definitely reorder from People Tree.

Title: A disappointment
Text:
Hi Katarina

We're very sorry you have not had a pleasant experience with your items.

Please note you can return bra tops. It is only underwear bottoms and earrings that we cannot accept returns on for hygiene reasons. Of course, if you receive something with a mark or stain we are extremely happy to return or replace this for you. Please contact our customer service team at people@peopletree.co.uk if you would like to discuss this.

In terms of our plastic packaging, I completely understand your concern. The plastic we use is made from polypropylene and is recyclable and biodegradable. We have two aeration holes in the plastic packaging to prevent condensation from forming, and the plastic is also Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT) free, which prevents garments from yellowing.

We use recyclable plastic packaging because in accordance with our commitment to sustainability we employ sea-freight for the majority of our products. This means we have to pack them in plastic to protect them from moisture damage in transit. This is not a remote scenario, unfortunately, and we have in the past lost parts of our collection due to damage in transit. This is a terrible shame for both our producers and customers. Any damage caused to the garments would result in unsellable product.

At the moment we are working on an alternative solution.

We are happy for customers to contact us after they have placed an order and to request it to be sent plastic free, if you wish to shop with us again in the future.

Next, I got in touch with peopletree and kindly asked if they could keep an eye out for the item in case of a return after Christmas but I was informed that they don't offer that service. Again, disappointing.
Overall, I'm a bit shocked by this poor customer service and I think it doesn't go with the company's image nor with the price range they work in. Bearing in mind that I didn't receive the item in the first place was due to an error on their behalf, I can't think of another retailer that let me down like that.
